rulebook.actual_reform_definition: Actual reform requires a legal or policy instrument, an explicit adopted measure, a parameter change, or an implementation update tied to a named reform, law, resolution, strategy, program, project, master plan, roadmap, or adopted package.

include_rules.label: Legal or regulatory change
include_rules.description: Include legal or policy instruments only when the text identifies an adopted or amended law, decree, resolution, regulation, order, code, legal act, or rule package.

include_rules.label: Adopted policy measure
include_rules.description: Include explicit adopted measures, approvals, launches, introduced packages, expanded incentives, or entered-into-force changes.

include_rules.label: Monetary or financial-sector parameter
include_rules.description: Include monetary or financial-sector parameter changes, not previews, analytical material, or future policy-rate meetings.

include_rules.label: Fiscal, tax, budget, subsidy, or tariff measure
include_rules.description: Include explicit fiscal, tax, budget, subsidy, duty, tariff, incentive, compensation, or fiscal-monitoring measures with an adopted or parameterized change.

include_rules.label: Trade or customs modernization
include_rules.description: Include adopted trade, customs, border, declaration, WTO, or market-access measures, not general trade or customs news.

include_rules.label: Structural implementation program
include_rules.description: Include implementation updates tied to a named reform, law, resolution, strategy, master plan, roadmap, program, project, or adopted package.

include_rules.label: Binding international financing or agreement
include_rules.description: Include signed grants, loans, and donor financing only when tied to a named adopted reform, program, master plan, or roadmap with implementation measures.

exclude_rules.label: Routine meeting without policy measure
exclude_rules.description: Exclude meetings, talks, roundtables, visits, and side-event diplomacy unless the text states a legal, budgetary, regulatory, or implementation measure.

exclude_rules.label: Cooperation news without instrument
exclude_rules.description: Exclude generic cooperation, partnership, or prospect-expansion announcements without a signed instrument, financing amount, or concrete policy action.

exclude_rules.label: Training or outreach
exclude_rules.description: Exclude trainings, calendars, awareness weeks, seminars, workshops, and public outreach even when they refer to an existing reform or resolution.

exclude_rules.label: Analytical report only
exclude_rules.description: Exclude analytical reports, indicator reviews, monitoring notes, and statistics releases without an adopted measure or implementation update.

exclude_rules.label: Ceremonial or cultural event
exclude_rules.description: Exclude commemorative, cultural, spiritual, award, and protocol events.

exclude_rules.label: Administrative update only
exclude_rules.description: Exclude internal performance-discipline, reporting, staffing, and ministry process updates without a policy measure.

exclude_rules.label: Generic announcement without action
exclude_rules.description: Exclude previews, future discussions, draft consultations, generic announcements, and published materials without an adopted measure or parameterized instrument.

relevance_scoring.hard_gate: Domain relevance alone is insufficient. A candidate must match at least one include rule and at least one hard reform signal: instrument, adopted measure, parameter change, named implementation update, or binding financing program.
relevance_scoring.high_relevance: 70-100: explicit adopted legal, regulatory, budget, monetary, trade, binding financing program, or implementation measure.
relevance_scoring.medium_relevance: 50-69: hard reform candidate with a single adopted instrument, measure, parameter, implementation, or binding financing program signal.
relevance_scoring.low_relevance: 0-49: generic news, routine activity, reports, training, cooperation, consultations, or insufficient hard reform evidence.

exclusion_reasons.description: The text does not identify a legal, regulatory, fiscal, monetary, trade, implementation, or financing measure.
The item is routine meeting or diplomatic engagement coverage without an explicit policy measure.
The item announces cooperation or prospects but no signed instrument, financing commitment, or policy change.
The item is training, awareness, outreach, or event logistics only.
The item is an analytical report, indicator review, monitoring note, or statistics release without an adopted reform measure.
The item is ceremonial, cultural, commemorative, or protocol content.
The item is internal administrative reporting without a policy measure.
The item previews future activity, consultation, or generic priorities without an adopted measure, instrument, parameter change, or named implementation update.
The text matched weak signals but not enough evidence for intake.
The item did not expose a stable item-level source link.

reform_packages.title: Healthcare quality, licensing, and private-sector participation reform
responsible_institutions: Agency for Attracting Investments and Implementing PPP Projects in Medicine
reform_packages.legal_basis: Official presidential instruction package on healthcare quality, licensing, accreditation, and private-sector participation.
reform_packages.official_basis: Official website of the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan, 2026-05-01.
reform_packages.financing_or_incentive: 200 billion soums preferential credit resources; loans up to 10 billion soums
reform_packages.short_summary: Tracks a presidential instruction package for healthcare licensing, accreditation, state-funded service eligibility, private-sector participation, preferential credit, and medical PPP delivery. The dated milestones are treated as implementation commitments from the cited source, not as an independently verified legal registry.
parameters_or_amounts: Loans up to 10 billion soums for private healthcare participation
Republican medical institution licensing deadline on 2027-04-01
Insurance Fund purchasing from accredited organizations from 2028
District/city medical association licensing target by 2030-12-31
reform_packages.why_tracked: The official source sets dated licensing, accreditation, state-funded service eligibility, credit, and institutional implementation milestones that affect health-sector service delivery and public financing channels.

implementation_milestones.label: Insurance Fund accreditation purchasing rule starts

official_source_events.title: Healthcare system development issues discussed
official_source_events.source_institution: Official website of the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan
official_source_events.summary: President Shavkat Mirziyoyev reviewed measures for the further development of the healthcare system, enhancement of the quality of medical services, and expansion of private sector participation. Consistent efforts are underway nationwide to provide the population with high-quality, modern medical care, improve management efficiency in the sector, make broader use of private sector potential, and elevate regional healthcare infrastructure to a new level. The role of the private sector in advancing the industry, along with the opportunities available in this area, was also examined. reform_packages.caveat: Automatic official-source tracker entry assembled from a verified source event. It is not an official legal registry and does not assert legal force beyond the cited official source.

reform_packages.title: Urbanization, construction permits, and housing delivery reform
reform_packages.policy_area: Urbanization, construction permits, housing, and infrastructure delivery
reform_packages.next_milestone: Construction permit and utility connection simplification starts
reform_packages.legal_basis: Official presidential meeting coverage on urbanization, master plans, construction permits, land privatization, and housing delivery.
reform_packages.official_basis: Government portal of the Republic of Uzbekistan, 2026-04-15.
reform_packages.financing_or_incentive: Annual master-plan budget allocations and 1.4 trillion soums planned infrastructure support for Yangi Uzbekistan residential areas identified in source.
reform_packages.short_summary: Tracks a presidential instruction package on urbanization, construction permitting, utility connection simplification, land-privatization processing, state-client KPIs, and regional housing delivery. The tracker separates dated implementation steps from the broader policy narrative in the source.
parameters_or_amounts: Single application and single payment for utility specifications from 2026-07-01
Draft resolution to reduce requirements, timelines, and payments by at least half
1.4 trillion soums planned infrastructure support for Yangi Uzbekistan residential areas
reform_packages.why_tracked: The official source sets dated instructions for master-plan preparation, online land-privatization processing, construction-permit simplification, utility-connection simplification, state-client KPIs, and housing delivery targets.

implementation_milestones.label: single-stage permit and utility-connection application starts
draft resolution on reducing requirements, timelines, and payments due

official_source_events.title: Progress and priorities in housing construction and urbanization reviewed
official_source_events.source_institution: Government portal of the Republic of Uzbekistan
official_source_events.summary: More than 500 industry representatives, including entrepreneurs, construction professionals, designers, architects, and urban plannersparticipated via regional studios. The Head of State emphasized that the construction sector plays a special role in achieving the ambitious goals set to ensure high economic growth across all sectors and regions. According to studies, each dollar invested in construction can generate an additional two to three dollars for the economy, while each job created in the sector leads to up to five new jobs in other industries. Over the past nine years, nearly 10,000 new construction enterprises have been established in the country. Twenty large companies with an ann

reform_packages.caveat: Automatic official-source tracker entry assembled from a verified source event. It is not an official legal registry and does not assert legal force beyond the cited official source.

reform_packages.title: Agriculture financing and subsidy delivery reform
reform_packages.policy_area: Agriculture financing, subsidies, and digital delivery
reform_packages.legal_basis: Official presidential presentation coverage citing the decree on improving state support for agriculture and the Agricultural Payments Agency.
reform_packages.official_basis: Government portal of the Republic of Uzbekistan, 2025-12-09.
reform_packages.financing_or_incentive: 34.2 trillion soums planned for cotton and grain harvest financing; 1.3 trillion soums of 2026 subsidies to be provided proactively; additional 5 trillion soums proposed for agrotechnical measures.
reform_packages.short_summary: Tracks an agriculture support package covering harvest financing, proactive subsidy delivery, the Agricultural Payments Agency, and digital subsidy administration through Agroportal and Agrosubsidy. Amounts are shown as source-reported envelopes, not as disbursement verification.
parameters_or_amounts: 34.2 trillion soums planned for cotton and grain harvest financing
1.3 trillion soums of 2026 subsidies to be provided proactively
Additional 5 trillion soums proposed for agrotechnical measures
Digital subsidy workflow through Agroportal and Agrosubsidy
reform_packages.why_tracked: The official source identifies a new agriculture support delivery system, quantified subsidy and financing envelopes, the Agricultural Payments Agency, and digitalized subsidy procedures through Agroportal and Agrosubsidy.

implementation_milestones.label: 34.2 trillion soums cotton and grain harvest financing plan
1.3 trillion soums proactive subsidy delivery through Agrosubsidy

official_source_events.title: Financing and subsidy plans for the agricultural sector reviewed
official_source_events.source_institution: Government portal of the Republic of Uzbekistan
official_source_events.summary: The presentation began with an overview of the funds allocated to the sector this year and their effectiveness. It was noted that 29 trillion soums were allocated for financing cotton and grain cultivation, purchasing agricultural machinery, and implementing water-saving technologies. Furthermore, 2,35 trillion soums in subsidies were directed to the sector. Allocating subsidies equivalent to 10 percent of the cotton value to 3,430 farms and clusters enabled them to cultivate cotton on 151 thousand hectares using their own funds, resulting in savings of 1,9 trillion soums. Moreover, farmers who fully repaid their loans for cotton cultivation this year were offered a reimbursement of 4 percen

reform_packages.caveat: Automatic official-source tracker entry assembled from a verified source event. It is not an official legal registry and does not assert legal force beyond the cited official source.

reform_packages.title: Tax administration and investment incentive reform
reform_packages.next_milestone: No future milestone published in verified source
responsible_institutions: Ministry of Investment, Industry and Trade of the Republic of Uzbekistan
reform_packages.legal_basis: Verified official source event: Uzbekistan Expands Tax Incentives for Investors Financing Infrastructure Projects.
reform_packages.official_basis: Ministry of Investment, Industry and Trade of the Republic of Uzbekistan
reform_packages.financing_or_incentive: Tax incentives for investors financing infrastructure projects
reform_packages.short_summary: Tracks a verified official-source event on tax incentives for investors financing infrastructure projects. The current source confirms the incentive measure but does not publish a future implementation deadline, so the tracker records the source event without inferring a forward milestone.
parameters_or_amounts: Tax incentives for investors financing infrastructure projects
Verified official source event published on 2026-04-14
No future implementation deadline was published in the extracted source
reform_packages.why_tracked: The verified official source records a fiscal incentive measure for investors financing infrastructure projects. It is tracked as an incentive package while avoiding any inferred implementation deadline not present in the source.

implementation_milestones.label: tax incentive source event recorded
responsible_institutions: Ministry of Investment, Industry and Trade of the Republic of Uzbekistan
